component.name: VideoFrame
component.description: Rounded thumbnail container with a fixed 16:9 aspect ratio and a centred semi-transparent play-button overlay. Purely presentational — no playback logic. Props: src (thumbnail URL) and alt.
component.figmaNodeId: note: uses text styles only; no Figma component set. Code Connect requires Figma Enterprise.

when: Displaying a video course preview with a recognisable play affordance
The content is a non-interactive thumbnail that represents a video — clicking is handled by the parent

patterns.name: video-preview-card
patterns.description: A VideoFrame above a title and description, used to preview a course video.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using VideoFrame for non-video image content
antiPatterns.reason: The play overlay signals video content; using it for static images misleads users.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use the Image component directly for non-video thumbnails.

antiPatterns.reason: VideoFrame is purely presentational. Adding state and media controls couples it to a specific player implementation.
antiPatterns.alternative: Wrap VideoFrame in a parent that handles click events and video playback separately.

states.description: Thumbnail image with centred play overlay. If src is not provided, renders a neutral placeholder background.

ariaAttributes: alt — pass a meaningful description of the video content; the play overlay is aria-hidden
accessibility.notes: VideoFrame itself is not interactive. The parent should handle click and keyboard events to initiate playback, using an appropriate role (e.g. button) on the wrapper.
